title: Value at Risk and Conditional Value at Risk
description: Master VaR and CVaR calculations using parametric, historical, and Monte Carlo methods. Understand why CVaR is a coherent risk measure while VaR is not.
learningObjectives: Calculate Value at Risk using parametric and historical methods
Understand CVaR as a coherent risk measure
Compare VaR and CVaR in portfolio risk management
Apply VaR/CVaR to portfolio optimization constraints
